content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-7-max

## Summary

Reviewed the additive adapter test work on feat/9ni46-adapter-tests. Verified via `cargo test --test unit br_adapter_test` and `cargo test --test unit bv_adapter_test`: 19 br tests and 8 bv tests all pass. Confirmed no source changes via `git diff master..HEAD -- src/` (zero diff). `cargo fmt --check` passes. Clippy warnings surfaced by `cargo clippy --tests -- -D warnings` are all pre-existing in the codebase and not introduced by the new test files. Coverage meets the bead: happy-path tests (show/ready/list-by-status/dep-tree/graph/create/update-status/close/sync-flush/sync-import plus dep add/parent-child/remove) and error-path tests (BrNotFound, BrTimeout, BrExitError with stdout/stderr/command preserved, BrParseError with raw_output, truncated JSON, sync_flush failure preserving dirty marker + journal record + has_pending_mutations) on the br side; robot-next and robot-triage parsing, plus BvNotFound, empty-graph parse error, blocked-graph parse error, BvExitError, BvParseError, BvTimeout on the bv side. All tests use a scripted `ProcessRunner`/`BvProcessRunner` mock that captures args, so command-line construction is verified. Mutation-journal/legacy-marker invariants are asserted against tempdir state. Assertion messages are descriptive. The bv empty/blocked-graph tests land as parse errors rather than a dedicated `no-next-bead` signal, but that accurately reflects the current adapter contract under the additive-only constraint and the tests explicitly document `not a panic`. No correctness, safety, or maintainability issues found. No amendments proposed.

## Amendments

None.
